items.title: Ex-Miss Universe Nigeria Chidimma Adetshina appears in S/African court to fight deportation
items.description: Former Miss Universe Nigeria 2024, Chidimma Adetshina, has appeared before the Cape Town Regional Court to challenge the Department of Home Affairs’ effort to deport her from South Africa. According to reports, the court appearance followed an application by Adetshina’s legal team contesting the lawfulness of the deportation process by the country’s Department of Home Affairs. Adetshina was arrested in June after Home Affairs alleged that she entered South Africa illegally and was later brought before court as the legal battle continues over possible deportation. The court proceedings mark the latest development in the immigration dispute surrounding the former Miss South Africa finalist. Adetshina, born in Soweto to a Nigerian father and a Mozambican mother, had initially contested for the Miss South Africa 2024 crown before withdrawing from the competition amid controversy surrounding her family’s citizenship status. Her withdrawal followed an investigation by South African authorities into allegations concerning her mother’s identity and citizenship documentation. In August 2024, the Department of Home Affairs stated that Adetshina’s mother may have been involved in identity theft. The controversy also sparked widespread social media attacks on Adetshina because of her Nigerian heritage, in spite of the fact that she was born in South Africa and met the eligibility requirements for the beauty pageant. She later accepted an invitation to participate in the Miss Universe Nigeria pageant, where she emerged winner and represented Nigeria at the Miss Universe 2024 competition. Adetshina finished as first runner-up at the global pageant and was crowned Miss Universe Africa and Oceania, becoming one of Nigeria’s most recognised beauty queens on the international stage. The affidavit she is expected to submit before the court is anticipated to outline the efforts she has made to regularise her stay in the country as she seeks to prevent her deportation. (NAN) The post Ex-Miss Universe Nigeria Chidimma Adetshina appears in S/African court to fight deportation appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Ismaïla Madior Fall ouvre le bal des procès des anciens ministres
items.description: L'ancien ministre de la Justice sous le régime du Président Macky Sall, Ismaïla Madior Fall, comparaît, aujourd'hui, devant la Haute Cour de justice. Il est le premier ex-membre du gouvernement à être jugé par cette juridiction dans le cadre des dossiers instruits à la suite de la deuxième alternance. Inculpé pour tentative de corruption, Ismaïla Madior Fall a été placé sous assignation à résidence sous surveillance électronique à l'issue de son audition devant la Commission d'instruction de la Haute Cour de justice. Cette affaire est liée à une parcelle de 9.598 m² située à Guédiawaye, initialement affectée au ministère de la Justice pour la construction d'un vaste complexe judiciaire. Le projet devait comprendre un Palais de justice, un centre de casiers judiciaires, un centre médico-social ainsi qu'un centre de surveillance pour bracelets électroniques. Selon les éléments du dossier, le protocole d'accord prévoyait que les travaux soient financés sur fonds propres par l'entrepreneur Cheikh Guèye. En contrepartie, une portion de l'assiette foncière devait lui être transférée en guise de paiement. Cheikh Guèye accuse, toutefois, l'ancien ministre d'avoir exigé un pot-de-vin de 250 millions de FCfa en échange de la signature dudit protocole. Il affirme avoir remis une première somme de 50 millions de FCfa en espèces dans le bureau d'Ismaïla Madior Fall, en présence de Mouhamed Anas El Bachir Wane. Dans la même affaire, ce dernier et Cheikh Guèye sont poursuivis pour escroquerie sur les deniers publics, corruption, association de malfaiteurs et blanchiment de capitaux. A. DIOUF

items.title: Diamond smile: Singapore jails Indian man for gem in mouth theft
items.description: A Singapore court sentenced an Indian tourist to more than two years in jail over a failed bid to steal a diamond by hiding it in his accomplice’s mouth. Mangroliya Manojkumar Kurjibhai, 41, and his accomplice visited a jewellery store in Singapore’s Chinatown on June 19 with their eyes on a 4.95-carat diamond worth $154,000, according to court documents seen by AFP on Tuesday. Mangroliya distracted the shop manager while his partner, 30-year-old Serasiya Milan Ramnikbhai, allegedly swapped the diamond with a fake hidden in his mouth, local media reported. The two men then left the store without buying anything, with Serasiya now carrying the real stone in his mouth. The theft was caught on surveillance camera, and the pair were arrested at the airport later that day – with the stolen gem recovered from one of their bags. Mangroliya was sentenced to two years, two months and two weeks in jail after pleading guilty last week, court papers said. Serasiya is in remand and scheduled for a hearing on Friday. The pair reportedly arranged for a counterfeit diamond — matching the real gem’s specifications and serial number — to be made in India before travelling to Singapore. It is unclear how they obtained the diamond’s details in advance. Earlier this month, jewellery worth millions of euros was stolen in a burglary at the museum of French luxury glassmaker Lalique, the company and an investigation source said. The thief or thieves broke into the museum in Wingen-sur-Moder in northeastern France around 5:30am (8:30am PKT) and headed straight for the jewellery room, the source close to the investigation said. “Around twenty pieces of jewellery were stolen. The loss is currently being assessed but could amount to several million euros, likely close to four million,” the source said. The museum said on its website that it will be closed for several days because of the burglary. “An alarm went off, but by the time the security company had completed its checks, it was a cleaning lady who arrived first on the scene and called the police,” the investigation source added. The CCTV footage is currently being examined.

items.title: Breaking: CBN retains MPR at 26.5%
items.description: The Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) has retained the Monetary Policy Rate (MPR), also known as benchmark interest rate, at 26. 5 per cent. CBN Governor Olayemi Cardoso announced this on Tuesday after the 306th Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting. “The Committee decided as follows: retain the monetary policy rate at 26.5 per cent,” Mr Cardoso said at the MPC briefing. This decision marks the second consecutive retention of the MPR at 26.5 per cent, following a 50-basis-point reduction in February from 27 per cent. “The MPC decision followed a thorough assessment of the balance; although headline inflation moderated marginally in June 2026, it has heightened due to renewed hostilities in the Middle East,” Cardoso said. The MPC also adjusted the asymmetric facilities corridor around the MPR to +50/-450 basis points—a move aimed at discouraging banks from keeping idle funds with the CBN and encouraging increased lending into the economy. Furthermore, the committee maintained the Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR) for commercial banks at 45 per cent, retained the rate for merchant banks at 16 per cent, and kept the CRR on non-TSA public-sector deposits at 75 per cent for liquidity management considerations. The interest rate was held after Nigeria’s headline inflation rate eased slightly to 15.91 per cent in June, down from 15.93 per cent recorded in May, according to the National Bureau of Statistics (NBS). The post Breaking: CBN retains MPR at 26.5% appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: RDC : l’opposition suspend sa mobilisation et fixe un ultimatum au pouvoir
items.description: En Republique democratique du Congo, l'opposition a decide de reporter la marche prevue le 22 juillet a Kinshasa contre le projet de modification de la Constitution. La decision a ete annoncee le 20 juillet par la plateforme C64, qui regroupe plusieurs formations politiques opposees au pouvoir de Felix Tshisekedi. Ce report a ete decide apres les discussions engagees avec les representants religieux, notamment la Conference episcopale nationale du Congo (Cenco) et l'eglise du Christ au Congo (ECC). Le 17 juillet , le president Felix Tshisekedi leur avait confie la mission d'organiser un dialogue national, repondant ainsi a l'une des principales revendications de l'opposition. Apres plusieurs reunions, les dirigeants de la C64 ont accepte de suspendre leur mobilisation, tout en affichant leur mefiance envers le pouvoir. « On a besoin de garanties », expliquait recemment un responsable de la coalition, alors que l'opposition affirme vouloir eviter une escalade des tensions. Dans un communique, la plateforme dit prendre acte de l'initiative presidentielle et estime que ce dialogue doit permettre d'aborder « les causes profondes de la crise » que traverse le pays, notamment l'insecurite dans l'Est, les difficultes economiques et sociales, ainsi que les problemes de gouvernance. L'opposition pose cependant un ultimatum au chef de l'etat. Elle demande a Felix Tshisekedi de convoquer officiellement le dialogue national avant le 15 août. a defaut, la C64 menace de relancer des « actions citoyennes pacifiques » pour defendre, selon elle, « l'ordre constitutionnel, la democratie et l'etat de droit ». Les responsables de la coalition appellent leurs militants a rester mobilises et demandent au pouvoir d'eviter toute provocation dans les prochains jours. L'un des principaux points de tension reste la loi referendaire adoptee en juin, qui pourrait ouvrir la voie a une modification de la Constitution.

items.title: National development requires strong values and collective action – Julius Debrah
items.description: Chief of Staff at the Presidency, Julius Debrah, has underscored the importance of strong national values, integrity and collective responsibility as essential pillars for Ghana’s sustainable development. Speaking at the National Development Conference organised by The Church of Pentecost, Mr Debrah described the event as a timely opportunity for national reflection on the principles that should shape the country’s development agenda. He said he was honoured to join the leadership of the Church and distinguished traditional leaders at the conference, where he conveyed the warm greetings and commendation of President John Dramani Mahama for what he described as a significant national initiative. Mr Debrah stressed that the progress of any nation could not be measured solely by economic growth and infrastructure development, but also by the values upheld by its citizens and institutions. According to him, Ghana’s true advancement depends on the character of its people, the integrity of its public institutions, and the commitment of leaders to serve with honesty, accountability and responsibility. “As Ghana continues to pursue sustainable development, the role of institutions such as the Church and Traditional Authorities remains indispensable,” he said. He noted that religious and traditional institutions serve as custodians of the country’s values, culture and social cohesion, adding that their partnership with government is crucial to building a nation founded on peace, unity and shared responsibility. Mr Debrah commended the Chairman and leadership of The Church of Pentecost for convening the National Development Conference, describing it as an important platform for dialogue and national reflection. He emphasised that achieving the Ghana citizens aspire to requires the collective efforts of all sectors of society, guided by a renewed commitment to integrity, selfless service and the common good.

items.title: Ambassador to SheaPark Resource Hub Project celebrates Shea Day with Buipe Wura
items.description: The Ambassador to the SheaPark Resource Hub Project and the Paramount Queen Mother of the Sing Traditional Area, Pognaa Salma Chaana Abdul-Razak, has joined Buipewura, Jinapor II, Paramount Chief of the Buipe Traditional Area, to celebrate Shea Day in Buipe. According to her, the annual observance should serve not only as a celebration of the shea tree but also as a renewed commitment to conserving it for future generations. She called for urgent action to protect Ghana’s shea trees, warning that the future of one of the country’s most valuable natural resources depends on the collective efforts of government, traditional authorities, development partners and local communities. The event, hosted by the Buipewura, Jinapor II, Paramount Chief of the Buipe Traditional Area, brought together chiefs, queenmothers, representatives of the Speaker of Parliament, Alban Bagbin, officials of the Global Shea Alliance, the Forestry Commission, women shea processors and stakeholders across Ghana’s shea value chain. Addressing attendees, Dr. Joseph Appiah-Gyapong, Director in-charge of climate change at the Forestry Commission, highlighted the growing threats facing the shea ecosystem across West Africa. “Across West Africa, we are losing an estimated eight million shea trees every year. Population growth, agricultural expansion, bushfires, unsustainable tree removal and the impacts of climate change are placing unprecedented pressure on our shared resources,” he said. He noted that if the current trend continues, projections indicate that global demand for shea products will soon outstrip supply, posing significant environmental and economic risks for countries that depend on the industry. Speaking at the event, the Ambassador also urged stronger collaboration among traditional authorities, government institutions, development partners and local communities to safeguard the future of the shea sector. “There is no project that can succeed without the involvement of traditional leaders. Community engagement begins with our chiefs and queenmothers, and their leadership remains critical to protecting our natural resources,” she stated. Representing the Right Honourable Speaker of Parliament, Alban Sumana Kingsford Bagbin, Founder and Chairperson of the Eco-Groove Development Initiative Foundation, the rep. congratulated the organisers and reaffirmed the Speaker’s commitment to supporting sustainable development within Ghana’s shea industry. He said strengthening partnerships and increasing investments in the sector would create jobs, improve livelihoods and contribute significantly to climate resilience. “Together, we can create thousands of decent jobs for our youth. Together, we can empower more women to build sustainable livelihoods. Together, we can restore degraded landscapes, strengthen our fight against climate change and position Ghana as the global benchmark for premium, sustainably produced shea products,” she said. The Ambassador also underscored the central role women continue to play in the shea value chain, describing them as the industry’s backbone whose contributions have sustained families and communities for generations. She encouraged participants to actively participate in nationwide tree planting initiatives and announced that, through the SheaPark Resource Hub Project, more than 30,000 shea seedlings have already been planted in the Upper West Region. “As ambassadors of the SheaPark Resource Hub, we have already planted over 30,000 shea seedlings. Today, I encourage every Ghanaian to plant more trees and help secure a greener future for generations to come,” she said. She further called on communities to work closely with the Global Shea Alliance to access improved shea seedlings and technical support to increase restoration efforts across northern Ghana. Speaking on behalf of the Global Shea Alliance, Idi Zakaria, described the shea tree as one of the region’s most valuable environmental and economic assets, stressing the need for governments, traditional authorities, research institutions, development partners and the private sector to work together to secure its future. He noted that the shea tree has long supported livelihoods across Northern Ghana by providing food, income and dignity to thousands of women and families, adding that protecting the species is essential for sustainable community development. “As we celebrate Shea Day today, let us remember that the future of shea depends on the choices we make now. This is not simply an environmental concern; it is an economic and social imperative,” he said. The celebration also focused on educating young people about the importance of preserving traditional values and conserving the shea tree to ensure future generations inherit a thriving ecosystem. The event concluded with a renewed commitment by stakeholders to strengthen conservation efforts, promote sustainable harvesting practices and expand investments in Ghana’s shea sector as part of broader efforts to combat climate change and improve livelihoods across the northern regions.

items.title: La faim dans le monde résiste, l'Afrique en première ligne
items.description: Quelque 645 millions de personnes ont souffert de la faim l'an dernier, soit 7,8% de la population mondiale, une légère amélioration qui risque de se heurter cette année aux chocs de la guerre au Moyen-Orient et du climat, estime mardi la FAO. La situation est particulièrement critique en Afrique, où 20% de la population est sujette à une sous-alimentation – c'est-à-dire un manque chronique de calories -, indique ce rapport annuel. Globalement, la crise économique post-Covid a plongé dans la faim des millions de personnes supplémentaires: 688 millions d'êtres d'humains étaient concernés en 2022. Depuis, la situation s'est redressée, sans retrouver toutefois les niveaux de 2019, selon l'Organisation pour l'alimentation et l'agriculture, agence de l'ONU. En 2030, l'ONU projette entre 510 et 520 millions de personnes sous-nourries, dont plus de la moitié en Afrique. « Le centre de gravité de la faim s'est déplacé de l'Asie jusqu'en Afrique », décrit à l'AFP David Laborde, directeur de la division économie agroalimentaire à la FAO. « Il y a 25 ans, la Chine avait encore un problème de faim, ils l'ont réglé; l'Inde a aussi un problème mais ils mettent beaucoup d'efforts pour le régler activement ». Les chocs climatiques de plus en plus sévères sont ressentis particulièrement durement en Afrique. « Des conflits comme le Soudan, ce qui se passe dans la région sahélienne, en République démocratique du Congo, pèsent aussi lourdement sur des gens qui ne peuvent avoir accès à leur terre ou n'ont plus de revenus », poursuit M. Laborde. Quant à la crise économique post-Covid, « on a commencé à récupérer, mais moins vite qu'on ne le pensait », dit-il. Selon ce rapport, 2,69 milliards de personnes – près d'une personne sur trois – ne peuvent s'offrir une alimentation saine, les coûts ayant crû de 25% en cinq ans. L'obésité chez les adultes continue elle de croître, à 16,2% de la population en 2024 (et 18,6% projetés en 2030). Cette année des événements, dont la guerre au Moyen-Orient, « menacent de saper les progrès » en Afrique et en Asie, alerte le rapport. La FAO se dit aussi « vigilante » quant à l'impact du phénomène océanique El Niño sur les récoltes, en Inde, en Afrique ou dans le « corridor sec » de l'Amérique du Sud, déjà très vulnérables face à l'insécurité alimentaire. The post La faim dans le monde résiste, l'Afrique en première ligne appeared first on Hespress Français - Actualités du Maroc .

items.title: Le HCR annonce la mort ou la disparition de 144 migrants au large de la Mauritanie
items.description: Quelque 144 réfugiés et migrants sont morts ou portés disparus au large des côtes mauritaniennes la semaine dernière, lors de tentatives de traversée irrégulière entre l’Afrique de l’Ouest et l'Europe, a indiqué mardi le Haut-Commissariat des Nations unies pour les réfugiés (HCR), qui déplore une nouvelle tragédie qui met en évidence le coût humain persistant de cette route migratoire parmi les plus meurtrières au monde. Selon le HCR, trois opérations de sauvetage et de débarquement menées entre les 14 et 18 juillet à Nouadhibou ont permis de secourir 387 personnes, l'Agence onusienne ayant participé à l'ensemble des opérations aux côtés des autorités mauritaniennes et de ses partenaires humanitaires afin d'assister les survivants et d'identifier les personnes vulnérables nécessitant une protection internationale. L'une des embarcations secourues le 18 juillet a connu un drame particulièrement meurtrier, avec 143 personnes présumées mortes ou disparues et seulement 38 survivants après près de 25 jours de dérive dans l'océan Atlantique à la suite de leur départ de Bufalo en Gambie, a précisé Matthew Saltmarsh, un porte-parole du HCR, lors d’un point de presse à Genève. Parmi les survivants figurent deux enfants ayant perdu tous les membres de leur famille durant la traversée et actuellement pris en charge dans un hôpital. Un autre décès a également été signalé parmi les passagers d'une embarcation débarquée le 14 juillet. Le HCR rappelle que la route atlantique reliant l'Afrique de l'Ouest aux îles Canaries demeure l'un des corridors migratoires mixtes les plus meurtriers au monde en raison des longues distances, des conditions océaniques imprévisibles, des embarcations précaires et surchargées ainsi que des difficultés d'accès à des opérations de secours rapides. L'Agence relève que, malgré une baisse de 61% des arrivées aux Canaries depuis le début de l'année par rapport à 2025, avec quelque 4.400 arrivées enregistrées au 15 juillet, les réfugiés et migrants continuent d'être exposés à des risques extrêmes, nombre d'entre eux entamant leur traversée à plus de 2.000 kms de leur destination. Depuis le début de l'année, plus de 40.000 arrivées par voie maritime ont été enregistrées en Europe sur les principales routes migratoires, contre 65.407 au cours du premier semestre de 2025. Le HCR appelle à redoubler d'efforts pour s'attaquer aux causes profondes des déplacements forcés et de la migration irrégulière, tout en renforçant les voies légales d'accès à la protection, notamment à travers l'augmentation des programmes de réinstallation, des possibilités de migration de travail et des opportunités d'éducation. Depuis le début de 2026, dix-sept opérations de débarquement ont été recensées à Nouadhibou et Nouakchott, permettant le sauvetage de 2.147 personnes. The post Le HCR annonce la mort ou la disparition de 144 migrants au large de la Mauritanie appeared first on Hespress Français - Actualités du Maroc .

items.title: La Guardia Civil descarta que la mujer muerta en Benahavís (Málaga) haya sido asesinada
items.description: La Guardia Civil investiga la muerte de una mujer que ha sido hallada este martes con signos de violencia en una vivienda de Benahavís (Málaga). Según han informado a EFE fuentes de la investigación, la víctima presenta un corte en el cuello por arma blanca. Sobre este caso, el Ministerio de Igualdad ya ha apuntado que se investiga como violencia machista, al igual que otro en Almoradí (Alicante). Y ha confirmado ya como crímenes machistas el de Alameda de la Sagrada, en Toledo, y el de Antequera, en Málaga. Seguir leyendo

items.title: Sudan’s Health Minister: Cholera outbreak in North Kordofan ‘contained’
items.description: Sudan’s Federal Minister of Health, Dr Haitham Mohamed Ibrahim, says cholera cases in the Mazroub area of North Kordofan have declined compared with recent weeks, while only a small number of cases have been recorded in Sheikan locality, with no evidence of wider transmission. Speaking to Radio Dabanga , Dr Ibrahim says Sudan declared itself free of cholera in January before new cases emerged in May in parts of West Kordofan under the control of the Rapid Support Forces (RSF). He says daily official reports submitted through the national disease surveillance system from all Sudanese states, including those under RSF control, prompted health authorities to collect and analyse samples, confirming cholera infections in a number of localities in West Kordofan. Dr Ibrahim says health officials responded immediately by directing organisations operating in the area, particularly the Kafa Organisation and Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF), to establish isolation centres in several West Kordofan localities. Daily surveillance and monitoring of cases were also introduced. He says population movements, particularly in war-affected areas, contributed to the spread of the disease after infected people travelled to Mazroub, where large numbers of cases were subsequently recorded. An isolation centre was established there, while international organisations and aid agencies provided medical supplies in coordination with North Kordofan state authorities, who also arranged for additional supplies to be delivered to the area. Dr Ibrahim says surveillance and daily monitoring continue, with the number of cholera cases in Mazroub now lower than in previous weeks. He adds that only limited cases have been reported in Sheikan locality and that there is no indication the disease is spreading there. Share article The post Sudan’s Health Minister: Cholera outbreak in North Kordofan ‘contained’ appeared first on Dabanga Radio TV Online .

items.title: Niger : arrivée d’un premier convoi algérien de 7 camions pour trois projets
items.description: La coopération entre l’Algérie et le Niger s’accélère et ne se limite pas au secteur de l’énergie. De nombreux projets de développement sont pris en charge dans ce pays du Sahel via l'Agence algérienne de coopération internationale, conformément à l’approche de l’Algérie, qui considère que la stabilité de la région passe par le développement économique et social. L'Agence algérienne de coopération prend en charge trois projets au Niger La télévision nigérienne a fait état, de l'arrivée dimanche 19 juillet, d’un premier convoi de sept gros camions d'Algérie transportant des équipements et du matériel de construction pour la réalisation d’infrastructures dans la région d’Agadez. Il s’agit d’un centre d'études islamiques, d’un centre d’hémodialyse et d’une polyclinique. Les trois projets seront réalisés par l’entreprise publique GCB (Génie Civil et Bâtiment), filiale de Sonatrach. “C’est avec une grande fierté que nous annonçons l’arrivée du convoi algérien pour le démarrage des travaux de réalisation des trois projets inscrits dans le cadre de la coopération algéro-nigérienne”, a déclaré sur place, à l’arrivée des sept camions, le directeur du projet au niveau de GCB, Mohamed Abidet. La coopération entre l'Algérie et le Niger va au-delà de l’énergie Ces projets entrent dans le cadre de la concrétisation des “importantes décisions stratégiques” prises lors de la visite du président Abdourahmane Tiani en Algérie en février dernier, a indiqué pour sa part le gouverneur d’Agadez Ibra Boulama Issa. Les premiers travaux ont déjà commencé, a souligné la télévision nigérienne. Après une crise qui a suivi le coup d'État à Niamey en juillet 2023, les relations entre les deux pays voisins sont revenues à la normale. L'Algérie et le Niger ont lancé cette année de grands projets énergétiques, dont le Gazoduc TSGP et la réalisation d’une centrale électrique de 40 mégawatts à Niamey, mise en chantier en mars dernier et inaugurée en juin en présence du Premier ministre Sifi Ghrieb. Les deux pays ont également convenu d'exploiter conjointement le champ pétrolier d’Afar, dans le nord du Niger. L’Algérie a aussi repris des relations normales avec le Mali après plusieurs années de crise, avec le retour cette semaine à leur poste des deux ambassadeurs respectifs et l’ouverture réciproque de l'espace aérien des deux pays. L’article Niger : arrivée d'un premier convoi algérien de 7 camions pour trois projets est apparu en premier sur TSA .

items.title: Kumasi Evangel Choir donates GH¢21,000 to support child cancer care at KATH
items.description: The Kumasi Evangel Choir, a choral group in the Ashanti Region, has donated GH¢21,000 to support cancer care for children at the Paediatric Oncology Unit of the Komfo Anokye Teaching Hospital (KATH). The donation fulfils a pledge made during the group’s recent “Total Praise Concert” held in Kumasi, where funds were raised to support children living with cancer and improve access to treatment. According to the group, the concert generated GH¢32,000, with the proceeds dedicated to supporting paediatric cancer care. “We are also deeply grateful to our patron, Nana Afia Achiaa Deiwaa, Queenmother of Asanteman in Sweden, whose generous contribution of an additional GH¢11,000 helped facilitate the discharge of patients who were unable to settle their medical bills,” the group said. The Kumasi Evangel Choir said the gesture forms part of its commitment to giving back to society and supporting vulnerable groups through its music ministry. The group expressed appreciation to patrons, sponsors, partners, supporters and fans whose contributions helped make the initiative possible. “Every ticket purchased, every donation made, and every act of support contributed to bringing hope and relief to children and families in need,” it added. The Paediatric Oncology Unit at KATH provides specialised care for children diagnosed with cancer, with support from individuals, organisations and development partners often helping to address treatment-related challenges faced by families.

items.title: Suspect in German mass shooting kills himself in jail
items.description: A man suspected of fatally shooting six child welfare workers at a shelter for mothers in Stade in northern Germany last month died by suicide in jail on Tuesday, officials said. The suspect, who has been partially identified in German media as 45-year-old Fatih Khan G., was found dead by jail staff in his single cell early Tuesday, the Lower Saxony state justice ministry said in a statement. The ministry said there are “currently no indications of third-party involvement” in the suspect’s death...

items.title: Ogou/Environnement : La DRE Plateaux-Est reboise le site de ses locaux
items.description: Atakpamé, 21 juil. (ATOP) – Les agents de la Direction régionale de l’éducation (DRE) Plateaux-Est ont reboisé la cour de leur direction, le mardi 21 juillet à Atakpamé. L’activité se situe dans le cadre de la promotion et la protection de l’environnement pour le développement durable. L’objectif est d’offrir un cadre de travail verdoyant et apaisant aux agents tout en rendant ce service public plus accueillant pour les usagers et visiteurs. Au total, 151 jeunes plants de diverses essences ont été mis en terre sur l’esplanade principale de la DRE, les bordures de la voie de désenclavement récemment aménagée et la cour intérieure du service. Pour ce troisième espace, le personnel s’est particulièrement mobilisé en apportant individuellement des plants floraux afin de constituer un bac à fleurs collectif. Le préfet de l’Ogou, Ekpé Kodjo Agbéko, a souligné que cette action s’inscrit en parfaite cohérence avec la vision environnementale et la politique nationale de reboisement insufflées par le gouvernement et le Président du Conseil, Faure Gnassingbé. Le directeur régional de l’éducation des Plateaux-Est, Ouro Bagna Fousseni, a exhorté l’ensemble de ses collaborateurs à s’investir non seulement dans la plantation, mais surtout dans le suivi et l’entretien régulier de ces plants. Il a ajouté que la mise en terre des plants constitue une empreinte positive et durable laissée par chaque agent au service de la collectivité et de l’administration. ATOP/KKT/MG/KYA

items.title: UNILAG begins Post-UTME July 27, warns candidates against malpractice
items.description: The University of Lagos (UNILAG) has warned candidates sitting for its 2026-2027 Post-Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination Aptitude Test against examination malpractice and violation of guidelines. The warning is in a statement issued on Tuesday by the university’s registrar and secretary to council, Mrs Abosede Wickliffe. She stated that the online aptitude test would be held from July 27 to July 31 under remotely proctored examination conditions. She said candidates must strictly comply with all examination instructions, warning that violations could attract disqualification or other disciplinary sanctions. She also said that candidates must use only laptops with in-built webcams and microphones throughout the examination. She added that mobile phones, tablets, external webcams, smart glasses, headphones and other electronic devices were prohibited during the test. The registrar said candidates must remain alone in a quiet, well-lit environment throughout the 30-minute examination. She added that speaking, reading questions aloud or allowing unauthorised persons into the examination area would constitute misconduct. She advised candidates to ensure stable internet connectivity and uninterrupted electricity supply throughout the examination. She noted that the university would not be liable for disruptions caused by power outages, network failures or other technical issues beyond its control. She added that candidates experiencing technical difficulties should immediately use the “Need Help?” feature on the examination platform. The university registrar said participation in the mock examination, available until July 25, was mandatory for all candidates. She explained that the mock examination would test device compatibility, internet connectivity, webcam functionality and overall system readiness. The registrar added that the Safe Examination Browser supports only Windows 10 or later and macOS Ventura or later operating systems. She urged candidates to use the same laptop, internet connection and examination environment for both mock and actual examinations. The registrar also warned candidates against patronising unauthorised individuals, cybercafés, business centres or CBT centres claiming affiliation with UNILAG. She stressed that the aptitude test would be conducted strictly online from locations chosen by candidates. She said the only approved fee for the screening exercise is ₦5,500, comprising ₦2,000 for the application and ₦3,500 for administrative fees. She advised candidates to obtain information only through the university’s official website and verified communication channels. The university registrar warned that fraud, impersonation, unauthorised assistance or examination malpractice could lead to cancellation of applications or examination results. She reiterated that all decisions relating to the conduct and outcome of the Post-UTME Aptitude Test would be final and binding. (NAN) The post UNILAG begins Post-UTME July 27, warns candidates against malpractice appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Are walkouts in parliament truly democratic?
items.description: The late Minister for Environment once made a memorable remark on the floor of Parliament before his untimely passing that “The Minority has staged more walkouts than their total numbers in Parliament.” While the comment was delivered with irony, it raises an important question worth examining: Are the frequent walkouts in Ghana’s Parliament strengthening or weakening our democracy? As a student of politics, democracy, and governance, I find myself reflecting on the increasing use of parliamentary walkouts, particularly by the New Patriotic Party (NPP) Minority Caucus in the current Ninth Parliament. Walkouts, much like demonstrations, are legitimate democratic tools. They allow individuals or groups to express dissatisfaction, register protest, and signal that they disagree with the direction of proceedings. However, an important question often goes unasked: does every Member of Parliament participating in these walkouts genuinely support the decision to leave the chamber? Or are some compelled by party discipline and the whip system to demonstrate solidarity, even when they may privately disagree? A parliamentary walkout is, at its core, a symbolic act. It occurs when members conclude that their participation is either unwanted or unlikely to influence the outcome of deliberations. By withdrawing their physical presence, they send a political message to both Parliament and the public. In that sense, walkouts have democratic value. They are a peaceful way of expressing dissent and avoiding confrontational exchanges. They communicate disagreement without disrupting parliamentary order. Yet democracy is not only about protest; it is also about persuasion. When lawmakers leave the chamber, Parliament loses the opportunity to hear alternative viewpoints, challenge assumptions, and improve legislation through rigorous debate. Silence, however symbolic, can never fully substitute for well-reasoned argument. The Ninth Parliament has witnessed several notable walkouts by the Minority Caucus, including those surrounding the passage of the GH¢1 levy and, more recently, the Tribunal Bill. The events of July 16, 2026, however, deserve particular attention. During proceedings, Minority Leader Hon. Alexander Afenyo-Markin was heard telling a colleague, “I take responsibility for this,” as he attempted to calm tensions within the caucus. Moments later, he informed the Speaker that we could not be part of the deliberations and the eventual passage of the bill. The choice of the word “we” is significant. Anyone who followed the parliamentary proceedings that day could also hear another Minority MP urging colleagues to remain in the chamber rather than walk out, arguing that they should stay and face the Majority. The Minority Leader reportedly responded by asking, What is our essence of being in the chamber? At the time, only 16 Minority MPs were present in the chamber. Observers also noted what appeared to be visible hesitation from Hon. Vincent Ekow Assafuah, suggesting that not every member was entirely convinced by the decision to leave. This raises another important question: was it the personal decision of the Minority Leader, or was it truly the collective decision of the entire NPP caucus? Such questions matter because parliamentary democracy thrives not only on party unity but also on individual conviction and robust deliberation. Walkouts may serve as a legitimate cry for attention, but they can also diminish the very institution they seek to influence. The constitutional responsibility of the Minority is to scrutinise the Majority, hold the government accountable, and present alternative arguments on behalf of the citizens who elected them. That responsibility is best fulfilled inside the chamber, not outside it. Press conferences certainly have their place in democratic politics, but they cannot replace debate on the floor of Parliament. Parliament is the nation’s official house of record. Every speech delivered, every argument advanced, and every objection raised becomes part of Ghana’s democratic history. Those contributions often carry greater weight than statements made after proceedings have concluded. Ghana’s democracy continues to mature. As it does, we must ask ourselves whether our parliamentary practices are evolving to deepen democratic accountability or merely reinforcing partisan loyalties. The whip system is an important feature of parliamentary governance, but it should not extinguish independent judgment or meaningful participation. The health of our democracy depends not only on the right to walk out but also on the willingness to stay, debate, persuade, and be persuaded. In Parliament, disagreement is not a weakness – it is democracy at work.

items.title: NPP Chairmanship race: Party yet to determine Wontumi’s fate
items.description: The campaign team of Bernard Antwi Boasiako, popularly known as Chairman Wontumi, is yet to decide whether to continue with his bid for the New Patriotic Party (NPP) national chairmanship race following his recent jail sentence. Former New Edubiase Member of Parliament, George Oduro, who disclosed this, said the team will first engage the NPP General Secretary and other stakeholders to determine the way forward for the campaign. Speaking in an interview on Adom FM’s Dwaso Nsem , Mr Oduro, who is also the Campaign Manager, said the team cannot immediately announce whether it will suspend or continue the campaign until the necessary consultations have been completed. “We cannot say we will stop the campaign or not. We have to engage the NPP General Secretary and ask that if something like this happens to an aspirant, what are the arrangements?” he said. He explained that he would personally visit Chairman Wontumi at the Nsawam Prison to discuss the matter with him and seek his views on the future of the campaign. “I will visit Wontumi at Nsawam today and have that discussion with him,” Mr Oduro added. According to him, the team expects to conclude consultations within a week and communicate its decision to party supporters and the general public. “In about a week, we hope to complete all necessary discussions and announce our decision,” he stated. Chairman Wontumi and his company, Akonta Mining Limited, were convicted by Justice Audrey Kocuvie-Tay on two counts each under the Minerals and Mining Act, 2006 (Act 703), as amended, and sentenced to 20 years’ imprisonment. However, the NPP has argued that the High Court erred by delivering the judgment in the absence of Wontumi’s lead counsel, Samuel Atta Akyea, who had informed the court that he would be outside the jurisdiction due to a prior professional engagement. Describing Wontumi as a political prisoner, the party has urged his legal team to pursue all available legal remedies, including an appeal and an application for certiorari, while assuring NPP supporters in the Ashanti Region and beyond that it would continue to challenge the conviction through constitutional means.

items.title: Barcelona agree €8.5m deal to sign Club Brugge winger Jesse Bisiwu
items.description: Barcelona have reportedly reached an agreement with Belgian side Club Brugge to sign highly rated youngster Jesse Bisiwu in a deal worth around €8.5 million. Transfer expert Fabrizio Romano reports that the Catalan giants have finally sealed the move after monitoring the 18-year-old winger for some time. Earlier reports suggested Barcelona had a €10 million bid rejected by Club Brugge. However, the two clubs have now agreed on a lower transfer fee, with the Belgian outfit securing a sell-on clause as part of the agreement. Bisiwu is regarded as one of Belgium’s brightest young prospects and has attracted attention for his pace, dribbling ability and attacking flair. The teenager is expected to begin his Barcelona career with Barca Atletic, although he could also earn opportunities with the senior team during the 2026/27 season. According to Mundo Deportivo, Bisiwu may join Hansi Flick’s first-team squad for the club’s upcoming pre-season training camp in England. Barcelona’s preparations for the new campaign have been affected by the absence of several players who are still unavailable following their involvement at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, giving Flick the chance to closely assess some of the club’s emerging talents during pre-season. Bisiwu is expected to be among the youngsters hoping to impress the German coach. The post Barcelona agree €8.5m deal to sign Club Brugge winger Jesse Bisiwu appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Lebanese army says troops deploying in ‘pilot zone’ after Israeli withdrawal
items.description: Lebanon’s army says its troops have begun deploying to a “pilot zone” in the south of the country following the withdrawal of Israeli forces under a US-brokered agreement reached last month. A statement from the army command said units had entered the village of Zawtar al-Gharbiyeh in Nabatieh district on Tuesday morning. On Monday night, Israel’s military confirmed the start of the pilot zone plan, which should see Israeli troops pulling out of a small part of the territory occupied during their conflict with Hezbollah as well as the disarmament of the Iran-backed group. It comes ahead of a meeting in Washington between US President Donald Trump and his Lebanese counterpart, Joseph Aoun. Aoun is expected to urge Trump to put pressure on Israel to withdraw from the rest of southern Lebanon and to provide support to the Lebanese army to take full control of areas where Hezbollah holds sway. The US state department announced on Monday afternoon that “pilot zone operations” had begun in Zawtar al-Gharbiyeh and two other Lebanese villages, Froun and Srifa, in line with the Trilateral Framework agreement between Israel, Lebanon and the US. It said the “milestone” was the direct result of discussions last week between Israeli and Lebanese representatives in Rome. Later on Monday, an Israeli military official confirmed that the Israel Defense Forces (IDF) would “adjust its force posture in one of the pilot areas” to allow the Lebanese army to carry out its mission, while also “safeguarding the security of IDF soldiers and preserving its right to self-defence and to remove threats”. “The objective of the pilot and the agreement is the disarmament of the Hezbollah terrorist organisation and the removal of its illegal weapons,” the official said. They also reiterated that the IDF would remain in the “security zone” – the 10km (six-mile) deep strip of occupied Lebanese territory running along the border – for “as long as necessary” to defend northern Israeli communities. On Tuesday, the Lebanese army’s command said its units had begun deploying to Zawtar al-Gharbiyeh “based on ongoing communications with the Military Co-ordination Group for Lebanon”, referring to the trilateral body created to support implementation of the framework. The statement also called on Lebanese citizens “not to head to the village until the security situation stabilises, and to comply with the instructions of the deployed military units for their own safety”. The Lebanese army said its troops were already carrying out missions in nearby Froun and Srifa following Monday’s US announcement. An Israeli source also told the Haaretz newspaper that there were no Israeli troops in those villages. Lebanon was drawn into the war between Israel, the US and Iran on March 2, when Hezbollah launched rockets into Israel in retaliation for a strike that killed Iran’s supreme leader. Israel responded by launching a bombing campaign across Lebanon and invading a significant part of the country’s south. More than 4,300 people have been killed in Lebanon, including almost 800 children, women and medics, according to the Lebanese health ministry. Israeli authorities say 37 Israeli soldiers and four civilians have been killed on both sides of the border during the conflict.

items.title: Two-thirds of people living with HIV are in sub-Saharan Africa – AU Commissioner
items.description: African Union (AU) Commissioner for Health, Humanitarian Affairs and Social Development, Amb. Amma A. Twum-Amoah, has raised concerns that nearly two-thirds of the world’s people living with HIV are in sub-Saharan Africa, despite significant progress in expanding access to life-saving treatment across the continent. Speaking at the ongoing AU Extraordinary Health Summit in Accra on Tuesday, July 21, she said millions of Africans living with HIV are now receiving life-saving antiretroviral treatment, describing it as a major achievement in the continent’s public health response. However, she stressed that the high concentration of HIV cases in sub-Saharan Africa underscores the need for sustained commitment and investment to tackle the epidemic and strengthen healthcare systems. “The unfinished agenda remains immense; nearly two-thirds of all people living with HIV are in Sub-Saharan Africa,” she noted. “Our continent has achieved hard-won progress. Millions of people living with HIV now have access to life-saving treatment,” she disclosed. “African institutions have demonstrated their capacity to lead coordinated responses to public health emergencies. These gains deserve recognition, but they cannot become an excuse for complacency,” she added. Amb. Twum-Amoah urged African leaders and development partners to intensify collaboration in addressing the continent’s health challenges, saying greater political will and collective action were essential to preventing future health crises and improving health outcomes across Africa. Ghana’s case Meanwhile, a recent report by the Ghana AIDS Commission disclosed that there is a decline in new HIV infections and AIDS-related deaths, with the latest national estimates showing progress in the country’s response to the epidemic. READ ALSO: Ghana records decline in new HIV infections and AIDS deaths in 2025 – AIDS Commission report The 2025 National HIV Estimates and Projections report by the Ghana AIDS Commission (GAC) indicates that new HIV infections reduced from 16,189 in 2024 to 13,951 in 2025. AIDS-related deaths also declined from 13,429 to 12,051 within the same period, representing nearly a 14% reduction. Presenting the report in Accra on Monday, July 20, the Director for Research, Monitoring and Evaluation at the Ghana AIDS Commission, Isaiah Doe Kwao, attributed the improvement to sustained investments in HIV testing, treatment and care services.

items.title: Nicaragua President Daniel Ortega cancels election after two decades in power
items.description: Nicaraguan President Daniel Ortega ruled out on Sunday any possibility of elections to prevent the opposition from taking office, after almost two decades in power. A firebrand Marxist in his youth and leader of a revolution against a US-backed autocrat, 80-year-old Ortega has been in power since 2007 following elections questioned by the international community. He is widely accused of acting harshly to crush dissent and remain in power in the poor Central American country. “They better forget it, here in Nicaragua there will be no more elections so they can try to grab the government and seize power,” Ortega said, referring to his opponents, during an event marking the 47th anniversary of the Sandinista revolution in the country’s capital Managua. Nicaragua was due to hold general elections in November, but a year and a half ago constitutional reforms extended the presidential term from five to six years, meaning the elections are now scheduled for 2027. Opposition movements living in exile hope international pressure will reinstate elections in Nicaragua. “They keep plotting… looking for ways to organize parties so that in an election, they’re assuming will happen, those parties would then win,” Ortega said before thousands of supporters, accusing his foes of being put in place by the United States. Ortega and his wife Rosario Murillo have ruled Nicaragua for nearly two decades with absolute power. They have kept a tight grip on society since a harsh crackdown on protests in 2018 left more than 300 people dead, according to the UN. The post Nicaragua President Daniel Ortega cancels election after two decades in power appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: La Jordanie, nouveau front stratégique dans le conflit entre Washington et Téhéran
items.description: Pour la premiere fois depuis le cessez-le-feu du 8 avril entre les etats-Unis et l'Iran, des soldats americains ont ete tues dans des frappes attribuees a Teheran. Deux militaires americains ont peri le 17 juillet dans une attaque balistique contre la base aerienne Muwaffaq Salti, en Jordanie, tandis qu'un troisieme est toujours porte disparu. Cette attaque marque une nouvelle etape dans l'escalade regionale et place le royaume hachemite au centre du conflit. Jusqu'ici, les frappes iraniennes visaient principalement Israel et les pays du Golfe. Mais la Jordanie apparaît desormais comme un nouvel objectif strategique pour Teheran. Selon plusieurs analystes, destabiliser Amman permettrait a l'Iran de fragiliser un allie majeur des etats-Unis dans la region et de mettre davantage de pression sur Israel, avec lequel le royaume a signe un traite de paix en 1994. 4 000 soldats americains sur place La multiplication des attaques contre le territoire jordanien intervient alors que Washington renforce son dispositif militaire dans le pays. Pres de 4 000 soldats americains y sont presents et des avions de combat supplementaires pourraient y etre deployes afin de limiter l'exposition des forces americaines dans le Golfe, devenu une cible privilegiee pour l'Iran. Cette evolution place toutefois les autorites jordaniennes face a une equation complexe. Amman depend fortement de l'aide americaine, notamment militaire et economique, mais doit egalement gerer une opinion publique largement hostile a la politique de Washington au Moyen-Orient, en particulier depuis la guerre a Gaza. Pour des experts, l'Iran chercherait a exploiter ces tensions internes en renforcant son influence. La Jordanie devient ainsi un enjeu majeur dans la confrontation indirecte entre Washington et Teheran, au risque de transformer le royaume en nouveau theâtre d'affrontement regional.

items.title: NDC rejects NPP’s ‘political prisoner’ claim, says Wontumi judgment proves wrongdoing
items.description: The governing National Democratic Congress (NDC) has dismissed the opposition New Patriotic Party’s (NPP) claim that Ashanti Regional Chairman Bernard Antwi Boasiako, popularly known as Chairman Wontumi, is a political prisoner following his conviction. Deputy Director of Elections and IT of the NDC, Rashid Tanko Computer, argued that the High Court’s judgment was based entirely on evidence presented during the trial and contained no indication of political persecution. Speaking in an interview on JoyNews’ PM Express on Monday, in response to the NPP’s criticism of the ruling, Mr Tanko challenged those questioning the judgment to read the court’s decision in full. “This is a ruling. The ruling is here. You read it. All those speaking – have they read the ruling? Tell me throughout the ruling whether you have seen any political issues in that. There is nothing. So where from this rendition of a political prisoner? Who is persecuting whom?” he asked. He accused the NPP of attempting to politicise a judicial process despite what he described as overwhelming evidence of wrongdoing. Mr Tanko urged that the judgment be translated into major Ghanaian languages, including Akan, Dagbani, and Hausa, among others, to allow the public to understand the court’s reasoning firsthand. “It will do us good as a country if we can buy airtime and air this ruling to the whole country. Translate it into Akan, Dagbani, Hausa, Kokomba and all the major languages in Ghana so that Ghanaians will see the ruling and determine whether the press conference they held today was not a cheap propaganda exercise,” he said. He further maintained that even witnesses associated with the prosecution had provided evidence linking the convicted NPP chairman to the alleged offences. According to Mr Tanko, one of the prosecution witnesses had led investigators to Kumasi to identify a person who allegedly supplied equipment connected to the case, stressing that the evidence was not disputed during the trial. His comments come after the NPP insisted that Chairman Wontumi’s conviction was politically motivated and described him as a victim of persecution after he was sentenced to 20 years in prison in an illegal mining case.

items.title: Nadra official goes missing in Karachi, family approaches police for his recovery
items.description: KARACHI: The family of a missing National Database and Registration Authority (Nadra) official approached police on Tuesday with an application for his recovery. The application, seen by Dawn , addressed Sindh Inspector General of Police (IG) Javed Alam Odho and stated that applicant Sohail Sangi’s younger son, Fayyaz Ahmed Janjhi, who is an assistant director at Nadra, had been missing since 6:30pm on July 20. Sangi, a veteran journalist and a member of the Human Rights Commission of Pakistan, further stated that his family had tried to contact Janjhi continuously, but his phone was switched off. The family also contacted his colleagues, who said he had left his office at Ayesha Manzil at around 6pm on Monday, he added. “But he did not reach home,” the application said. Urging authorities to register a complaint of him going missing and locate him, the applicant said his son or the rest of the family had no enmity with anyone. Janjhi’s elder brother, Riaz Sohail, who is also a senior journalist at BBC , told Dawn that the family had approached the Sindh IG with the application, and the provincial police chief had assigned the case to Central Senior Superintendent of Police Imran Khan. The Human Rights Commission of Pakistan (HRCP) also posted on X: “HRCP is deeply concerned to learn that Fayaz Janjhi, a Nadra officer and son of HRCP council member Sohail Sangi, has been missing since yesterday. We demand that the Sindh government expedite their efforts to recover Mr Janjhi safely.” Karachi Press Club President Fazil Jamili posted on X that he, too, was concerned about Janjhi’s disappearance and urged the Sindh government and law enforcement agencies to take “immediate action and expedite efforts to ensure his safe and swift recovery”.

items.title: BFEM: un taux de réussite de 53,38% au premier tour
items.description: Le premier tour du Brevet de fin d'études moyennes (Bfem) 2026 s'est soldé par un taux d'admission national de 53,38%. Les statistiques publiées par la Direction des examens et concours (Dexco) mettent en évidence des performances contrastées selon les académies. Si plusieurs régions de l'intérieur enregistrent des résultats remarquables, les grands pôles urbains continuent d'afficher des taux de réussite inférieurs à la moyenne nationale. Les premiers résultats de l’examen du Bfem 2026 confirment une dynamique globalement positive. Sur 195.664 candidats inscrits, 192.856 ont effectivement pris part aux épreuves, soit un taux de présence de 98,56%. Selon la Direction des examens et concours (Dexco) qui a communiqué au « Soleil » les résultats, au terme des corrections du premier groupe, 102.943 candidats ont été déclarés admis, représentant 53,38% des présents. Les filles continuent de devancer légèrement les garçons avec un taux de réussite de 54,02%, contre 52,46% chez leurs camarades masculins. Dans certaines académies comme Kaffrine ou Sédhiou, les garçons réalisent néanmoins des performances supérieures, preuve que les dynamiques restent variables, selon les contextes locaux. Au-delà de ces premiers résultats, les perspectives demeurent encourageantes. Les statistiques montrent que 31,96% des candidats sont autorisés à subir les épreuves du second groupe. Selon le Directeur des examens et concours, Papa Baba Diassé, cette configuration laisse apparaître, à ce stade, une tendance globale de réussite de 84%, contre 78,90% lors de la session 2025, soit une progression de 5,10 points. Une estimation qui devra toutefois être confirmée à l'issue des épreuves du second groupe et de la publication des résultats définitifs. L'analyse des résultats révèle, cependant, un paysage contrasté. Les meilleures performances sont enregistrées par les académies de Kolda, qui affichent un taux de réussite de 74,82%, Kédougou (74,49%), Matam (72,81%) et Kaffrine (70,22%). Ces académies confirment les progrès observés ces dernières années dans plusieurs régions de l'intérieur, où les efforts consentis en matière d'encadrement pédagogique et de suivi des élèves semblent produire des résultats significatifs. À l'inverse, les académies concentrant les effectifs les plus importants continuent de tirer la moyenne nationale vers le bas. Thiès, qui compte 34.875 candidats inscrits, n'enregistre qu'un taux de réussite de 44,07%. Pikine-Guédiawaye, avec plus de 27.000 candidats, obtient 44,40%, tandis que Rufisque (43,82%) présente le taux le plus faible du pays. Fatick (48,16%) demeure également sous la barre des 50%. L’Académie de Kolda en tête avec 74,82% de réussite Ces résultats rappellent les défis auxquels restent confrontés les grands bassins scolaires, où les effectifs élevés rendent souvent plus complexe le suivi pédagogique des élèves. Les autres académies affichent des performances plus proches ou supérieures à la moyenne nationale. Selon la Dexco, Tambacounda enregistre 65,79% de réussite, devant Sédhiou (64,07%), Ziguinchor (61,45%), Saint-Louis (59,67%), Kaolack (58,47%), Louga (54,54%), Dakar (54,41%) et Diourbel (54,02%). Cette répartition traduit des écarts parfois importants entre les territoires et souligne les marges de progression qui subsistent dans certaines académies. Selon la Dexco, les statistiques mettent également en évidence la forte mobilisation des candidats. Sur l'ensemble du territoire, seuls 2.808 candidats ont été absents, soit un taux de présence de 98,56%. Plusieurs académies, notamment Dakar, affichent même des taux de présence supérieurs à 99%, témoignant de la bonne organisation de l'examen et de l'engagement des candidats. Cette session marque également une avancée importante dans la modernisation de la publication des résultats. Selon le Directeur des examens et concours, Papa Baba Diassé, les traditionnelles scènes d'attroupement devant les centres d'examen appartiennent désormais au passé. Grâce au code QR intégré aux convocations, les candidats ont pu consulter leurs résultats directement depuis leur domicile, à l'aide de leur téléphone portable, mais aussi télécharger et imprimer leur relevé de notes en ligne. Cette innovation s'inscrit dans la stratégie de digitalisation des examens nationaux engagée par le ministère de l'Éducation nationale et vise à offrir un service plus rapide, plus sécurisé et plus accessible aux candidats. Le rendez-vous est déjà fixé pour les candidats admissibles. Dans un communiqué publié samedi dernier, le ministère de l'Éducation nationale annonce que les épreuves du second groupe se dérouleront le mardi 21 juillet 2026 à partir de 7 h 30 sur l'ensemble du territoire national, ainsi qu'en Gambie et en Arabie saoudite. Les candidats autorisés à y prendre part sont invités à se présenter dans leurs centres d'examen munis de leur convocation et d'une pièce d'identité en cours de validité. Cette ultime étape permettra de connaître le taux définitif de réussite de la session 2026 du Bfem. Daouda DIOUF

items.title: Michael Okpara varsity clears air over recruitment controversy
items.description: By Steve Oko The management of Michael Okpara University of Agriculture, Umudike (MOUAU), has firmly defended Vice-Chancellor, Prof. Ursula Akanwa, against allegations surrounding the controversial recruitment exercise, while blaming the mess on the immediate past management, which superintended over the exercise, for not restricting itself to the approved quota. It said Prof. Akanwa neither supervised nor influenced the recruitment process, insisting that the employment exercise had been concluded before she took over as Vice-Chancellor on March 1, 2026. The management further explained that the payroll capture of the successful candidates was not done by the university but by the office of the Accountant General of the Federation. The clarification comes amid growing concerns from some of the affected personnel, who said they were recruited in February 2026 and had been working without pay since then, only to have their identity cards retrieved during a meeting with the university management last week. According to them, they were also issued fresh letters at the meeting, designating them as “Surplus to Needs,” a development they said had left them uncertain about their employment status. “We don’t know whether we have been sacked or if we are still staff members,” one of the affected workers lamented. Speaking at a press conference in Umudike, the Head of the University’s Public Relations and Protocol Unit, Mr Anyaso Anyaso, described attempts to drag the current Vice-Chancellor into the controversy as deliberate misinformation by “mischief makers.” He said the facts clearly showed that the Federal Government approved the recruitment of 426 staff, a figure equally endorsed by the University’s Governing Council, which directed the then Vice-Chancellor and his management to strictly comply with the approved quota. According to him, the previous administration allegedly ignored that directive by issuing appointment letters to 618 persons, while 553 names were subsequently captured on the Government Integrated Financial Management Information System (GIFMIS). He explained that the Federal Government’s payroll platform eventually recognised only the 426 vacancies officially approved by the Government and the Governing Council, leaving the excess recruits outside the approved payroll. “The Vice-Chancellor has repeatedly made it clear at meetings with the university community that the recruitment exercise, including payroll capture, had already been completed before she assumed office. Anyone seeking explanations should direct such enquiries to those who conducted the exercise,” Anyaso stated. The management further dismissed allegations that Prof. Akanwa orchestrated the removal of names from the Federal Government payroll, stressing that the Vice-Chancellor has no access to the GIFMIS platform and therefore lacks the authority to upload, delete or alter any employee’s records. It maintained that the current administration merely inherited the consequences of an exercise carried out in excess of the approved recruitment quota and should not be blamed for decisions taken before Prof. Akanwa’s appointment. The university urged members of the public to ignore what it described as false narratives aimed at tarnishing the image of the Vice-Chancellor, insisting that official records clearly exonerate the current management from any wrongdoing. It also advised stakeholders to rely only on official university communications and refrain from spreading unverified reports capable of misleading the public. The clarification followed public outrage over the alleged disengagement of 192 staff, with the university maintaining that the development was the direct fallout of the immediate-past administration’s decision to recruit far beyond the 426 positions approved by the Federal Government and the Governing Council. It also denied sacking any staff, explaining that it was simply complying with directives that insisted that the university should not exceed its approved quota. The university management which expressed sympathy over the plight of the affected staff, said its hands were tied as there were no budgetary provisions to accommodate their salaries. The post Michael Okpara varsity clears air over recruitment controversy appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Shatta Wale is smart for using AI in music production – Perez Musik
items.description: Ghanaian gospel musician Perez Musik has thrown his support behind the use of artificial intelligence (AI) in music production, describing it as a valuable tool that is making the creative process easier and more efficient. Speaking on Daybreak Hitz with Kwame Dadzie and Doreen Avio, Perez Musik cited dancehall artiste Shatta Wale as an example of a musician who has smartly embraced AI in his music production. “Shatta is smart. I listen to a wide range of music because I am an artiste so when I saw Shatta Wale’s African King album I wanted to know what it’s about. There is one of the songs that he used the AI choir and it is beautiful,” he said. Perez stated that he also used AI for some aspects of his music production, although he didn’t rely solely on it. The musician further explained that he did not use AI-generated music as the final recording but rather as a demo to help shape his ideas before heading into the studio. “These days, AI has also become part of my creative process. For this particular song, I didn’t use AI, I used Logic Pro. But now, I don’t stress myself as much. Sometimes, when I get an idea for a song, I type it into an AI tool. If the output isn’t exactly what I want, I simply refine my prompt until it generates something closer to my vision,” he said on Hitz FM . “I don’t record the AI-generated song. I only use it to create a demo or sketch. In the past, we had to go to the studio just to record a rough demo. Now, you can create that initial sketch right on your phone,” he added. Perez Musik also highlighted that the effectiveness of AI depends on how well users communicate their ideas through prompts, detailing instructions to help generate better results. “The key is knowing how to prompt the AI properly. You have to tell it exactly what you want, where you want certain instruments, where you want the music to change, or how you want the arrangement to flow. Once you do that, it gives you a solid idea to work with,” he stated. While acknowledging concerns about the misuse of AI, the gospel musician maintained that technology should not be condemned, insisting that it could be used productively. “Using AI saves a lot of time and reduces stress before and during production. It’s helping us, and I see it as just another tool. We shouldn’t demonise it. Like every tool, it can be abused, but when it’s used responsibly, AI can be a very useful part of the creative process,” he added. Perez Musik is one of Ghana’s leading contemporary gospel musicians, known for songs such as Hewale Lala, Blema Tesaa, Aseda, Ayeyi and God of Wonders. His latest release, Oguamaa , featuring Cynthia Appiadu of Daughters of Glorious Jesus, continues his blend of contemporary gospel music with rich Ghanaian musical influences.

items.title: Patience: the virtue that protects continuity
items.description: One of the quiet changes that often accompanies age is patience. Many people discover that they become less hurried, less anxious for immediate results, and less inclined to force outcomes. They listen more. They react less. They wait more comfortably. Why? The common explanation is that age brings wisdom. While true, it does not fully explain why wisdom makes us patient. The Continuity Principle offers a deeper answer. Patience is confidence in continuity. It is the confidence that worthwhile outcomes emerge through connected actions over time. When we are young, life is largely an experiment. Every challenge feels new. Every setback feels permanent. Every opportunity seems fleeting. Without much accumulated experience, urgency feels rational because we have little evidence that time itself can be be an ally. As we grow older, something remarkable happens. We begin to accumulate evidence. We have watched storms pass that once seemed endless. We have seen broken relationships heal. We have watched children become adults. We have seen businesses recover from crises that appeared fatal. We have experienced investments compound, skills mature, and reputations strengthen—not overnight, but through years of consistent effort. Each experience becomes another piece of evidence that continuity works. Patience is born from that evidence. It is not passive waiting. It is informed waiting. It is active confidence that today’s faithful actions will become tomorrow’s visible results. This also sheds new light on an ancient saying: Patience is a virtue. Why has nearly every culture regarded patience as a virtue? Because patience protects continuity. Without patience, we interrupt the very processes that create value. We stop learning before mastery. We leave relationships before trust has deepened. We abandon businesses before they become sustainable. We give up on investments before they compound. We quit reforms before they transform institutions. In each case, the problem is not that continuity failed. The problem is that continuity was not given enough time to work. Seen through this lens, patience is far more than the ability to wait. It is the discipline to preserve continuity until its rewards become visible. This is why impatience often accompanies youth. It is not a character flaw. It is frequently the absence of accumulated proof. When you have not yet witnessed continuity producing extraordinary outcomes, speed appears to be the only path to progress. Age changes that. Older people have seen continuity accomplish what force never could. You cannot hurry trust. You cannot rush wisdom. You cannot accelerate character. You cannot compress the years required to master a craft. You cannot force a tree to mature by pulling on its branches. Some things respond only to continuity. The greatest achievements of humanity are not built through isolated moments of brilliance but through thousands of connected actions repeated over time. Scientific discoveries build upon earlier discoveries. Civilizations are built upon the work of previous generations. Families are strengthened through daily acts of love. Great companies earn trust one customer at a time. Every enduring success is, at its heart, an expression of continuity. Patience, therefore, is not the opposite of action. It is the companion of purposeful action. It allows us to continue planting when there is no harvest. It allows us to continue learning when there is no recognition. It allows us to continue serving when there is no applause. It allows us to continue believing when there is no immediate evidence. Without patience, continuity is abandoned before it has time to produce its rewards. Without continuity, patience has nothing to sustain. The two are inseparable. Perhaps this is why many older people appear calmer. They are not simply slowing down. They have learned to trust a force that younger people are only beginning to discover. They have seen continuity transform effort into mastery, relationships into families, ideas into institutions, and dreams into reality. That confidence cannot be taught quickly. It must itself be accumulated through continuity. So perhaps the real gift of growing older is not merely becoming wiser. It is becoming increasingly convinced that some of life’s greatest miracles cannot be rushed. Because continuity has already shown you what time can accomplish. Patience, then, is not simply a pleasant personality trait. It is the virtue that protects continuity. And continuity is the force that transforms today’s consistent actions into tomorrow’s enduring value.

items.title: Millions now access HIV treatment, but Africa’s HIV burden remains high – AU Commissioner
items.description: African Union (AU) Commissioner for Health, Humanitarian Affairs and Social Development, Amb. Amma A. Twum-Amoah, has commended the significant progress made in expanding access to life-saving treatment for millions of Africans living with HIV. Speaking at the ongoing AU Extraordinary Health Summit in Accra on Tuesday, July 21, she described the achievement as a major milestone in the continent’s efforts to combat HIV/AIDS. “Our continent has achieved hard-won progress. Millions of people living with HIV now have access to life-saving treatment,” she disclosed. “African institutions have demonstrated their capacity to lead coordinated responses to public health emergencies. These gains deserve recognition, but they cannot become an excuse for complacency,” she added. Despite the progress, Amb. Twum-Amoah noted that sub-Saharan Africa remains the epicentre of the epidemic, with nearly two-thirds of the world’s people living with HIV residing in the region. “The unfinished agenda remains immense; nearly two-thirds of all people living with HIV are in Sub-Saharan Africa,” she noted. She therefore called on African leaders and stakeholders to renew their commitment to addressing the continent’s health challenges, stressing the need for stronger collaboration and sustained investment to build resilient healthcare systems and effectively respond to future health crises.

items.title: Un joyero que mató a tiros a dos atracadores, nuevo héroe de la derecha italiana
items.description: Hay muchos nervios en la derecha italiana del Gobierno de Giorgia Meloni a un año de las elecciones, si no se adelantan a primavera, ante el ascenso del nuevo líder ultra, el exgeneral Roberto Vannacci , que acusa al Ejecutivo de ser muy blando y que en los sondeos ya obtiene un 7% de los votos, por encima de la Liga de Matteo Salvini. Por eso la competición surge ante cualquier hecho de actualidad que se preste a demostrar quién es más extremista. El último ha sido la condena definitiva a 14 años y nueve meses de prisión a un joyero de Grinzane Cavour, en la región de Piamonte, por matar a tiros en 2021 a dos asaltantes y herir a un tercero, a los que siguió con una pistola en la calle cuando huían y disparó por la espalda. Las imágenes grabadas en vídeo son claras. Seguir leyendo

items.title: KSE-100 makes marginal gains as global energy uncertainty weighs on early rally
items.description: The Pakistan Stock Exchange’s (PSX) benchmark index on Tuesday made marginal gains as investor sentiment remained cautious over uncertainty surrounding global energy markets. The KSE-100 index opened in the green, soaring by 2,442.72 points to reach an intraday high of 178,370.45 at 10:09am. However, the market dipped sharply from 12pm onwards, dropping below the 177,000-point level at 1:56pm. It then finally closed at 176,133.56 points, up by mere 205.83 points (0.12 per cent) from the previous close. Topline Securities Ltd noted that the “strong start proved short-lived, as participants opted to lock in gains at elevated levels, triggering broad-based profit-taking during the latter half of the session”. “The subdued close reflected cautious investor sentiment amid lingering geopolitical tensions and persistent uncertainty surrounding global energy markets, prompting market participants to remain selective despite the early optimism,” it added. Topline further observed that refinery stocks bore the brunt of the selling pressure amid “market speculation that the long-awaited refinery policy could face further delays”. “Overall, investor sentiment remained guarded as market participants continued to assess geopolitical developments and their potential implications for global oil prices, inflation, and foreign investment flows,” the brokerage house said. Earlier in the day, KSE-100 had witnessed a strong rally as markets weighed reports of mediation efforts between the US and Iran against an exchange of fresh attacks between the two. As of 1:15pm PKT, global oil prices had fallen by more than 1pc. However, the majority of the early market gains eroded as oil prices rose by nearly 2pc, following continued hostilities in the Gulf as well as threats of a naval blockade of Saudi Arabia by Yemen’s Houthis. Brent crude futures rose $1.62, or 1.8pc, to $90.84 a barrel by 4:52pm PKT. The front-month US West Texas Intermediate crude contract, which expires on Tuesday, was up $1.37, or 1.7pc, at $84.60. The more active contract for September delivery rose $1.48, or 1.8pc, to $83.96. Awais Ashraf, director of research at AKD Securities, said the market extended the previous session’s late recovery as “oil prices retreated from a one-month high amid renewed mediation efforts between the US and Iran”. “Investor sentiment is also being supported by optimism surrounding the ongoing earnings season, with oil and gas exploration, cement, refinery and textile companies expected to post stronger-than-usual results,” he said. The stock exchange had opened the week on Monday on a depressed note, with the market remaining under extreme volatility. However, late value-hunting allowed the benchmark KSE-100 index to close the session in the green with a meagre gain. Additional input from Reuters
